name: David "Fathead" Newman
abstract: David "Fathead" Newman (February 24, 1933 – January 20, 2009) was an American jazz and rhythm-and-blues saxophonist, who made numerous recordings as a session musician and leader, but is best known for his work as a sideman on seminal 1950s and early 1960s recordings by Ray Charles. The AllMusic Guide to Jazz wrote that "there have not b…

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him the last two years of his life and I always found him a great guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: We had ... David “Fathead” Newman on saxophone ... So it was a good tour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: I look at the film I did on Fathead [Newman] which is just a very short thing. I think that’s beautiful because it captured Fathead.
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Just almost made it into that Fathead [David Newman] style, almost, because that’s where he was coming from.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: I’m going down to North Texas State to do a tribute to David Newman, David “Fathead” Newman.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: David Newman... I mean all these guys were all legends to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003
